question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/MirrorNodeState.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: initQueueStates()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger concurrent EVM simulation requests that race on the shared unsynchronized LinkedList backing TRANSACTION_RECEIPTS_STATE_ID, causing transaction receipts to be inserted out of order and permanently reordering the visible transaction history for all users?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/MirrorNodeState.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: initQueueStates()] Can an unprivileged user submit two simultaneous eth_call or eth_estimateGas requests that both obtain a ListWritableQueueState wrapping the same LinkedList instance, then race on addToDataSource() so that receipt entries are interleaved in an attacker-controlled order, corrupting the canonical transaction receipt sequence?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/MirrorNodeState.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getWritableStates()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the computeIfAbsent on writableStates to obtain a stale WritableStates view for RecordCacheService that references an already-committed queue, then inject additional receipt entries into the committed queue, causing those entries to appear in the transaction history out of their original chronological order?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/MirrorNodeState.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getReadableStates()] Can an unprivileged user trigger the onCommit Runnable (readableStates.remove(serviceName)) via a commit() call on MapWritableStates, then immediately issue a getReadableStates() call for RecordCacheService before the queue is re-initialized, causing a new empty MapReadableStates to be cached and making all prior transaction receipts invisible to subsequent readers?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/MirrorNodeState.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getWritableStates()] Can an unprivileged user cause a TOCTOU race between the computeIfAbsent check on writableStates and the subsequent states.get(s) call on the non-thread-safe HashMap, resulting in a null serviceStates reference that returns EmptyWritableStates, silently dropping all pending transaction receipt writes and leaving the history incomplete?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/MirrorNodeState.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: initQueueStates()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that the LinkedList backing TRANSACTION_RECEIPTS_STATE_ID is shared across all ListWritableQueueState and ListReadableQueueState instances to perform a concurrent removeFromDataSource() that removes a legitimate receipt entry mid-iteration, causing ConcurrentModificationException or silently dropping a transaction from the visible history?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/MirrorNodeState.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getWritableStates()] Can an unprivileged user obtain a WritableQueueState for RecordCacheService via getWritableStates(), call addToDataSource() to inject a crafted receipt entry at the tail of the LinkedList, and then trigger a commit() that persists this injected entry into the shared queue, causing a fabricated transaction to appear in the transaction history without any corresponding on-chain transaction?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/MirrorNodeState.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getWritableStates()] Can an unprivileged user race two concurrent getWritableStates() calls for RecordCacheService such that both computeIfAbsent calls observe a missing entry simultaneously, resulting in two distinct MapWritableStates instances wrapping the same underlying LinkedList, and then both commit independently, causing double-application of receipt mutations and duplicating transaction history entries?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/MirrorNodeState.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getReadableStates()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the computeIfAbsent caching in readableStates to obtain a ListReadableQueueState whose iterateOnDataSource() returns a live iterator over the shared LinkedList, then trigger a concurrent addToDataSource() from another thread, causing ConcurrentModificationException that aborts a transaction history read mid-stream and returns a partial, inconsistent receipt list?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/MirrorNodeState.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: initQueueStates()] Can an unprivileged user submit a high volume of concurrent simulation requests that each call addToDataSource() on the shared LinkedList without synchronization, causing structural corruption of the LinkedList's internal node pointers and permanently scrambling the order of all transaction receipts stored in TRANSACTION_RECEIPTS_STATE_ID?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/MirrorNodeState.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getWritableStates()] Can an unprivileged user trigger the onCommit callback (readableStates.remove(serviceName)) at a moment when another thread is mid-way through iterating the cached ListReadableQueueState for RecordCacheService, causing the iterator to operate on a detached stale queue snapshot while new receipts are added to the live queue, producing a split-brain view of transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/MirrorNodeState.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getWritableStates()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that getWritableStates() internally calls getReadableStates(serviceName) to construct MapWritableKVState, creating a circular dependency where a commit()-triggered readableStates.remove() invalidates the readable cache that the writable state still holds a reference to, causing subsequent reads of transaction-related KV state to return stale pre-commit values and misrepresenting transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/MirrorNodeState.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: initQueueStates()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the non-thread-safe HashMap used as the inner map for RecordCacheService in the states field, by triggering concurrent initQueueStates()-adjacent writes and reads, causing the HashMap to enter an infinite loop or return null for TRANSACTION_RECEIPTS_STATE_ID, making the transaction receipt queue inaccessible and erasing visible transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/MirrorNodeState.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getReadableStates()] Can an unprivileged user cause the readableStates ConcurrentHashMap to cache a MapReadableStates built from a partially-initialized states entry (due to a race with initKVStates()), resulting in a ReadableStates view that is missing certain state IDs and causing transaction-related state lookups to throw IllegalArgumentException, disrupting the integrity of transaction history reads?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/MirrorNodeState.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getWritableStates()] Can an unprivileged user submit requests that cause MapWritableStates.commit() to be called multiple times on the same instance (since writableStates caches the instance via computeIfAbsent), causing the onCommit Runnable to remove and re-add the readable state cache multiple times, creating a window where transaction history is transiently empty and concurrent readers observe a gap in the receipt sequence?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/MirrorNodeState.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: initQueueStates()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the LinkedList's non-atomic peek-then-remove pattern in ListReadableQueueState.peekOnDataSource() and ListWritableQueueState.removeFromDataSource() to create a TOCTOU race where a receipt that was peeked by one thread is removed by another before it is processed, causing that transaction receipt to be permanently lost from the history?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/MirrorNodeState.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getWritableStates()] Can an unprivileged user trigger concurrent commit() calls on two different MapWritableStates instances that both wrap the same RecordCacheService queue, causing the onCommit Runnable to execute twice and remove the readableStates cache entry twice, with the second removal hitting an already-rebuilt cache and evicting a freshly-computed receipt list, making transaction history transiently disappear?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/MirrorNodeState.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getReadableStates()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the computeIfAbsent lambda in getReadableStates() which reads from the non-thread-safe states HashMap, by timing a concurrent modification to states (e.g., via initKVStates path) to cause the lambda to observe a partially-constructed inner map, resulting in a MapReadableStates that omits TRANSACTION_RECEIPTS_STATE_ID and hides transaction history from callers?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/MirrorNodeState.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: initQueueStates()] Can an unprivileged user submit enough concurrent requests to cause the shared LinkedList for TRANSACTION_RECEIPTS_STATE_ID to grow unboundedly (since there is no size cap enforced in addToDataSource()), and then exploit the unbounded queue to inject a large number of crafted receipt entries that push legitimate receipts to positions where they are never reached by bounded iteration, effectively hiding real transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/MirrorNodeState.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getWritableStates()] Can an unprivileged user obtain a ListWritableQueueState for RecordCacheService and call removeFromDataSource() repeatedly to drain the entire TRANSACTION_RECEIPTS_STATE_ID queue, causing all transaction receipts to be removed from the in-memory history without any authorization check, resulting in a complete erasure of the visible transaction receipt sequence?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/MirrorNodeState.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getWritableStates()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the absence of any access control check in getWritableStates() for RecordCacheService to obtain a writable handle to the TRANSACTION_RECEIPTS_STATE_ID queue and insert duplicate receipt entries, causing the same transaction to appear multiple times in the history and misleading downstream consumers about the true transaction count?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/MirrorNodeState.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getReadableStates()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that getReadableStates() wraps the live LinkedList in a ListReadableQueueState without copying it, so that a concurrent write to the queue via ListWritableQueueState.addToDataSource() is immediately visible to an ongoing iteration, causing a reader to observe a mid-flight receipt insertion and return an inconsistent, partially-updated transaction history snapshot?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/MirrorNodeState.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getWritableStates()] Can an unprivileged user cause the MapWritableStates onCommit lambda (readableStates.remove(serviceName)) to race with a concurrent getReadableStates() computeIfAbsent, such that the removal and re-insertion of the readable cache happen in an order that leaves a stale pre-commit ListReadableQueueState cached, causing subsequent readers to see the old transaction receipt ordering instead of the post-commit ordering?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/MirrorNodeState.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: initQueueStates()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that the LinkedList is initialized once in initQueueStates() and never reset between requests, so that receipt entries from one user's simulation bleed into another user's simulation context, causing cross-request contamination of transaction history and allowing one user to observe or influence another user's receipt sequence?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/MirrorNodeState.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getWritableStates()] Can an unprivileged user trigger a scenario where getWritableStates() for RecordCacheService returns a cached MapWritableStates from a previous request (due to computeIfAbsent not evicting), and then commit() on this stale instance re-applies old receipt mutations to the current queue, causing historical receipt entries to be re-inserted and disrupting the chronological order of transaction history?
